mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Excess inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) triggers hawkish monetary policy expectations, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in CPIAUCSL > 3% periods exceed 0.5% daily volatility or Rank IC > 0.15 in corresponding windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE signals tight labor markets, boosting corporate earnings visibility and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-moving PAYEMS/UNRATE regimes show negative or insignificant Rank IC (< 0.05) across 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in HOUST (housing starts) amid rising FEDFUNDS signals reduced consumer confidence, creating headwinds for equity markets and negative 20d forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in HOUST/FEDFUNDS co-contraction regimes exceed 1.0% or show positive Rank IC (> 0.1) in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO (industrial production) signals weakening supply-side capacity, creating deflationary risks that depress S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in INDPRO contraction periods exceed 0.8% or show positive Rank IC (> 0.1) in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS (consumer credit) combined with stable GDP growth implies overleveraged households, creating tail risks for equity markets and negative 60d returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in RSAFS/GDPC1 co-contraction regimes exceed 1.5% or show positive Rank IC (> 0.15) in 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE below 4% with stable PAYEMS indicates labor market overheating, prompting Fed rate hikes that drive negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in UNRATE/PAYEMS overheating regimes exceed 0.7% or show positive Rank IC (> 0.1) in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with declining FEDFUNDS suggests accommodative policy supporting supply-side expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in INDPRO/FEDFUNDS expansion regimes fall below 0.3% or show negative Rank IC (< -0.05) in 2-year windows.
